questions.q: Satish Dhawan is best remembered as the chairman of which Indian organisation?
questions.explanation: Satish Dhawan served as chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), guiding its launch-vehicle programme.

questions.q: In which field of engineering did Satish Dhawan primarily work?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was an aerospace engineer and pioneer of fluid dynamics and aeronautical research in India.

questions.q: India's main spaceport is named the Satish Dhawan Space Centre. In which town is it located?
questions.explanation: The Satish Dhawan Space Centre is the launch facility at Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan took over as ISRO chairman after the death of which founder of the Indian space programme?
questions.explanation: Dhawan succeeded Vikram Sarabhai, the founder of the Indian space programme, as ISRO chairman in 1972.

questions.q: The subtitle on Satish Dhawan's trivia card calls him the builder of what?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as the 'Builder of Indian Rocketry' for shaping India's launch-vehicle programme.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan was a leading figure at which Bengaluru institution, where he later became director?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was a faculty member and later director of the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) in Bengaluru.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan was a citizen and scientist of which country?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was an Indian scientist who led India's national space organisation, ISRO.

questions.q: Which type of vehicle did Dhawan's ISRO chiefly work to develop and launch from Sriharikota?
questions.explanation: Under Dhawan, ISRO developed satellite launch vehicles (rockets) to place Indian satellites into orbit.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan is widely described by which profession?
questions.explanation: He was an aerospace engineer who became one of India's most influential research administrators.

questions.q: The Satish Dhawan Space Centre is located on the coast of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Sriharikota, home of the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, lies on the coast of Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: In which year did Satish Dhawan become chairman of ISRO?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was appointed ISRO chairman in 1972 following the death of Vikram Sarabhai.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan earned his doctorate in aeronautics and mathematics under which famous aerodynamicist?
questions.explanation: Dhawan completed his PhD at Caltech under Hans W. Liepmann, a leading figure in aeronautics and fluid dynamics.

questions.q: At which American university did Satish Dhawan obtain his doctorate in aeronautical engineering?
questions.explanation: Dhawan earned his doctorate at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) in the United States.

questions.q: Besides being ISRO chairman, Dhawan simultaneously held which other top post for much of the 1970s?
questions.explanation: Dhawan continued as director of the Indian Institute of Science even while serving as ISRO chairman.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan was a pioneer of experimental research in which branch of physics relevant to flight?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was a pioneer of boundary-layer, turbulence and fluid-dynamics research, foundational to aeronautics.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan set up India's first supersonic facility of which kind at IISc?
questions.explanation: Dhawan established one of India's first and most important supersonic wind tunnels at the Indian Institute of Science.

questions.q: The space centre at Sriharikota was renamed the Satish Dhawan Space Centre after which event?
questions.explanation: The launch centre was renamed in his honour after Dhawan's death in 2002.

questions.q: Which of these high civilian honours did the Government of India confer on Satish Dhawan?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan is often credited with mentoring which SLV-3 project director and future President of India?
questions.explanation: Dhawan mentored A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, who led the SLV-3 project and later became President of India.

questions.q: When India's first satellite launch vehicle SLV-3 failed in 1979, Dhawan is famous for doing what?
options: Taking responsibility publicly for the failure
questions.explanation: Dhawan publicly took responsibility for the SLV-3 failure, and after the 1980 success let the team take the credit, a celebrated example of leadership.

questions.q: The first successful flight of the SLV-3, which placed the Rohini satellite in orbit, occurred under Dhawan in which year?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 successfully launched the Rohini satellite into orbit in 1980, making India a space-faring nation.

questions.q: In what year was Satish Dhawan born?
questions.explanation: Satish Dhawan was born in 1920 in Srinagar.

questions.q: Satish Dhawan was born in 1920 in which city?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was born in Srinagar in 1920.

questions.q: Before going to Caltech, Dhawan's early degrees included an unusual combination of engineering and which other subject?
questions.explanation: Dhawan held degrees in both mechanical engineering and the humanities, including an MA in English literature, before his aeronautics doctorate.

questions.q: Dhawan became director of the Indian Institute of Science in which year, the youngest to hold the post at the time?
questions.explanation: Dhawan was appointed director of the Indian Institute of Science in 1962.

questions.q: Dhawan championed using space technology for national development, including a famous 1975-76 experiment that beamed educational TV to villages. What was it called?
questions.explanation: The Satellite Instructional Television Experiment (SITE) used a satellite to broadcast educational programmes to rural India during 1975-76, reflecting Dhawan's development-focused vision.

questions.q: Dhawan accepted the ISRO chairmanship in 1972 only after which personal condition about his existing job was met?
options: That he be given a cabinet minister rank
That he be allowed to continue as IISc director
That he report directly to the President
questions.explanation: Dhawan agreed to lead ISRO on the condition that he could continue directing the Indian Institute of Science from Bengaluru, where ISRO headquarters were then relocated.

questions.q: While at ISRO, Dhawan also chaired which national body overseeing the country's space activities?
options: Space Commission and the Department of Space
questions.explanation: Dhawan served as chairman of the Space Commission and secretary of the Department of Space, the apex bodies for India's space policy.

questions.q: Dhawan's research at Caltech and IISc helped establish India as a centre for the study of which flow phenomenon, including transition and separated flows?
questions.explanation: Dhawan's experimental work focused on boundary-layer transition, turbulence and separated flows, building India's reputation in fluid mechanics.

questions.q: In what year did Satish Dhawan pass away, prompting the renaming of the Sriharikota launch centre in his honour?
questions.explanation: Dhawan died in 2002, and the Sriharikota launch facility was renamed the Satish Dhawan Space Centre that year.
